attack_chain.description: A DXC technical team member hardcoded AWS cryptographic keys (secure variables) into code being shared among multiple teams.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Various secure variables (cryptographic keys that allowed access to DXC procured Amazon Web Services resources) were hardcoded into a piece of work

evidence_chunks.quote: Various secure variables (cryptographic keys that allowed access to DXC procured Amazon Web Services resources) were hardcoded into a piece of work
Various secure variables (cryptographic keys that allowed access to DXC procured Amazon Web Services resources) were hardcoded into a piece of work

attack_chain.description: On September 27, the employee uploaded the code containing hardcoded AWS keys to a personal public GitHub repository, where unknown miscreants discovered the exposed credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: on September 27, a member of the technical team created a personal space on the public Github, and the code was loaded to this unsecured repository

evidence_chunks.quote: on September 27, a member of the technical team created a personal space on the public Github, and the code was loaded to this unsecured repository
on September 27, a member of the technical team created a personal space on the public Github, and the code was loaded to this unsecured repository

attack_chain.description: Attackers used the exposed private AWS keys to authenticate to DXC's AWS account.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the private keys were used to start 244 AWS virtual machines

evidence_chunks.quote: the private keys were used to start 244 AWS virtual machines
the private keys were used to start 244 AWS virtual machines

attack_chain.description: Over four days attackers spun up 244 EC2 virtual machines (most within 24 hours of key publication) to run cryptomining workloads, racking up a $64,000 AWS bill.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Over a period of four days, the private keys were used to start 244 AWS virtual machines. The cost incurred was $64k

evidence_chunks.quote: Over a period of four days, the private keys were used to start 244 AWS virtual machines. The cost incurred was $64k
Over a period of four days, the private keys were used to start 244 AWS virtual machines. The cost incurred was $64k

initial_access_vector: AWS private keys hardcoded in source code accidentally committed to a public GitHub repository and harvested by unknown miscreants.
persistence_mechanism: None observed; exposure window was under 24 hours before the repo was removed and keys rotated.
privilege_escalation: None required; the leaked AWS keys already had privileges sufficient to launch EC2 instances.
